core_mechanism: measurement-induced collapse of a superposition of hidden states via repeated two-path interferometric probing, where the pairwise distinguishability of the states (a path-dependent relative phase) governs whether collapse occurs and its rate, and the number of probes needed follows from sequential statistical hypothesis discrimination to a target confidence level
brief_reason: The abstract describes sending probes through the two legs of an interferometer so that superpositions of the hidden charge collapse precisely when the states are distinguishable (via monodromy), with that distinguishability also setting the collapse rate and the number of probes required for a desired confidence in discriminating the states.
